reasoning: The question asks for the probability that the daily average temperature at Toulouse-Blagnac Airport on the resolution date (presumably 2026-02-07, the question close date) will be higher than on 2025-11-09. Based on historically averaged climate normals from Météo-France for Toulouse, the average daily temperature around early November (specifically Nov 9) is roughly 10.5°C, as temps transition from the October mean of 15.0°C to the November mean of 9.7°C. For early February (Feb 7), the normal temperature is roughly 6.9°C, transitioning up from the January mean of ~6.4°C to the February mean of 7.2°C. This translates to an expected difference of approximately -3.6°C (February being colder). Given typical daily temperature standard deviations for these months in Toulouse (~3.5°C for November and ~4.0°C for February), the standard deviation of the temperature difference between the two dates is around 5.3°C. Using a normal distribution, the probability that a random daily average in early February exceeds one in early November is roughly P(Z > 3.6/5.3) = P(Z > 0.68) ≈ 25%. Although weather patterns vary heavily, standard climatological modeling suggests this represents the most historically grounded unconditioned distribution of outcoming events.
